name: google-calendar
description: Google Calendar scheduling, event lookup, creation, update, deletion, and free/busy handling.
goal: Translate natural-language schedule requests into safe calendar actions.
trigger_when: Use when the user asks to inspect or change schedule data.
skip_when: Skip when the task only mentions time casually without calendar action.
expected_output: Calendar action plan or executed calendar result.

name: unreal
description: Unreal Engine 5 C++, Blueprint, gameplay, AI, GAS, UI, and project integration work.
goal: Produce correct UE5-focused implementation guidance or code changes.
trigger_when: Use when the task explicitly targets Unreal Engine projects or systems.
skip_when: Skip when the work is unrelated to Unreal Engine.

name: code-general
description: General-purpose coding and implementation work across the current repository.
goal: Implement or modify code safely in the current project.
trigger_when: Use for repository changes, debugging, and backend implementation tasks.
skip_when: Skip when a more precise specialist clearly fits the task.
expected_output: Concrete code change, fix summary, or executable implementation result.

name: research
description: Investigation, comparison, fact gathering, and diagnostic analysis for technical tasks.
goal: Gather the most relevant facts before implementation.
trigger_when: Use when the task asks for investigation or comparison before coding.
skip_when: Skip when the request is clearly a direct code edit with no discovery needed.
expected_output: Focused findings with concrete evidence or next action.

name: document
description: Documentation, guides, summaries, and structured written deliverables.
trigger_when: Use when the task is primarily documentation or user-facing explanation.
skip_when: Skip when the task is mainly code implementation.
expected_output: Updated documentation or clear written output.

name: google-docs
description: Google Docs and document-oriented workspace tasks.
goal: Handle document-focused tasks that involve structured written content.
trigger_when: Use when the task explicitly references Google Docs style workflows.
skip_when: Skip when ordinary markdown or repository docs are enough.
expected_output: Structured document result or editing guidance.
